steps.title: Use the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content: The two given lengths are the legs of a right triangle, so the hypotenuse comes from $c^2=27^2+24^2$. Factor the result to match the form $3\sqrt{d}$.

steps.title: Factor the legs and apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Notice that both legs have a factor of 3: $27 = 3(9)$ and $24 = 3(8)$. Using the Pythagorean theorem:
$$c^2 = 27^2 + 24^2 = [3(9)]^2 + [3(8)]^2$$

steps.title: Simplify to find d
steps.content:
Expand the squared terms:
$$c^2 = 9(81) + 9(64) = 9(81 + 64) = 9(145)$$
Taking the square root:
$$c = \sqrt{9(145)} = 3\sqrt{145}$$
Comparing to $3\sqrt{d}$, we have $d = 145$.
